title: Ukraine formally asks Israel to seize vessel carrying grain stolen by Russia
content: A senior Ukrainian diplomatic source told Euronews that “if Israel responded to other requests via diplomatic channels, there would have been no need for tweets”, as Israeli FM Sa’ar lashed out at Kyiv over what he said was “Twitter diplomacy”.

title: Two people stabbed in north London area of Golders Green, Jewish groups say
content: The incident comes in the wake of a spate of arson attacks on synagogues and Jewish community sites in north London in recent weeks.
snippet: The incident comes in the wake of a spate of arson attacks on synagogues and Jewish community sites in north London in recent weeks.

title: Man accused of spying for Russia arrested in Berlin, prosecutors say
content: The suspect allegedly gave his Russian intelligence handler information about German military aid for Ukraine and photos of NATO military convoys moving along motorways.
snippet: The suspect allegedly gave his Russian intelligence handler information about German military aid for Ukraine and photos of NATO military convoys moving along motorways.

title: Kubilius pushes Europen Defence Union but critics brand it a distraction
content: EU defence chief Andrius Kubilius is pushing a European Defence Union to tie Ukraine with its European allies, but the idea is already under fire, with one MEP warning that it risks distracting from the current job of bulletproofing the EU.

title: 'The EU is not the little sister of the US,' European Parliament Vice-President Wilmès says
content: The EU and the US should talk to each other as equal partners, European Parliament Vice-President and former Belgian Prime Minister Sophie Wilmès told Euronews' morning show Europe Today.
snippet: The EU and the US should talk to each other as equal partners, European Parliament Vice-President and former Belgian Prime Minister Sophie Wilmès told Euronews' morning show Europe Today.

title: Ukraine says it will open arms exports with 'Drone Deals,' but not to all countries
content: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Tuesday that Ukraine is set to open its arms exports via so-called drone deals, but not all countries will have access to Kyiv's battlefield tested expertise.
snippet: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Tuesday that Ukraine is set to open its arms exports via so-called drone deals, but not all countries will have access to Kyiv's battlefield tested expertise

title: Hungary's incoming PM Péter Magyar offers to meet Ukraine's president in June
content: Relations between the two neighbours hit rock bottom before Hungary's 12 April election that saw Magyar beat long-time nationalist premier Viktor Orbán.
snippet: Relations between the two neighbours hit rock bottom before Hungary's 12 April election that saw Magyar beat long-time nationalist premier Viktor Orbán.

title: European Parliament demands €200 billion extra for EU budget in hardball talks
content: The European Parliament has adopted its position on the EU long-term budget, calling for a 10% increase and pushing for EU-level taxes. MEPs say they are ready for tough talks as money talks intensify for 2028-2034 cycle.
snippet: The European Parliament has adopted its position on the EU long-term budget, calling for a 10% increase and pushing for EU-level taxes. MEPs say they are ready for tough talks as money talks intensify

title: German bid to rescue 'Timmy' the whale stranded off Baltic coast passes key hurdle
content: The whale was first spotted stuck on a sandbank on 23 March before freeing itself and then becoming stuck again several times.
snippet: The whale was first spotted stuck on a sandbank on 23 March before freeing itself and then becoming stuck again several times.

title: Ukraine says it shot down 33,000 Russian drones in March, a new monthly record
content: Ukraine has developed cutting-edge and battle-tested drone technology that has proved essential in holding back Russia’s bigger army and has drawn military interest from around the world.
snippet: Ukraine has developed cutting-edge and battle-tested drone technology that has proved essential in holding back Russia’s bigger army and has drawn military interest from around the world.

title: Ukraine summons Israel's ambassador over allegedly stolen grain being shipped to Haifa
content: Kyiv has warned Israel of a brewing diplomatic crisis, should authorities permit a vessel carrying grain allegedly stolen from Russian-occupied Ukrainian territories to dock and unload.
snippet: Kyiv has warned Israel of a brewing diplomatic crisis, should authorities permit a vessel carrying grain allegedly stolen from Russian-occupied Ukrainian territories to dock and unload.

title: Fact-checking claims that Spain's legalised migrants can move to other EU countries
content: Under Sánchez's programme, undocumented migrants who have lived in Spain for at least five months can apply for a one-year temporary residence permit, which has opened the door to disinformation.
snippet: Under Sánchez's programme, undocumented migrants who have lived in Spain for at least five months can apply for a one-year temporary residence permit, which has opened the door to disinformation.

title: UN human rights office rebukes Sweden for deporting disabled child twice to Albania

title: Italy extradites alleged Chinese hacker to US accused of spying for Beijing during COVID-19 pandemic

title: Pope Leo XIV prays with Archbishop of Canterbury in historic encounter at Vatican
content: The papal audience at the Vatican comes in the 60th anniversary year of the first formal ecumenical statement between the Anglican and Roman Catholic Churches.

title: Drone crashes in Romania as Russia strikes neighbouring Ukraine
content: Two British fighter jets were scrambled and 200 people were evacuated on Saturday after a drone crashed in Romania during a barrage of Russian strikes on its neighbour Ukraine.
snippet: Two British fighter jets were scrambled and 200 people were evacuated on Saturday after a drone crashed in Romania during a barrage of Russian strikes on its neighbour Ukraine.

title: Montenegro inches closer towards EU membership
content: Montenegro, which has been an EU accession candidate country since 2010, hopes to become the European Union's 28th member by 2028.
snippet: Montenegro, which has been an EU accession candidate country since 2010, hopes to become the European Union's 28th member by 2028.

title: UK House of Lords accused of 'obstructionism' as time runs out on assisted dying bill

title: MEPs with new-borns can delegate their vote – but only if they are women
content: The European Parliament has approved a rule allowing proxy voting for new mothers before and after childbirth. However, it raises questions about gender equality, as it does not cover male MEPs.
snippet: The European Parliament has approved a rule allowing proxy voting for new mothers before and after childbirth. However, it raises questions about gender equality, as it does not cover male MEPs.

title: Second US ambassador to Ukraine steps down in less than one year

title: EU loosens state aid rules as Middle East crisis hits energy costs

snippet: Brussels loosens state aid rules as Middle East turmoil drives up fuel and fertiliser costs, allowing governments to cushion farmers, hauliers and industry with emergency subsidies.

title: EU backs ending new oil and gas drilling
content: The war in the Middle East may have driven up oil and gas demand, but the world should still ditch new fossil fuel drilling, EU climate chief Wopke Hoekstra has said, insisting on "affordable, reliable, homegrown clean energy."

title: King Charles III lauds ‘indispensable’ US-UK alliance at White House state dinner

title: Santorini announces new restrictions after scientists flagged volcanic activity

snippet: New restrictions, including the closure of a hiking trail, could remain in place until 2027 as scientists are concerned over volcanic and seismic hazards.

title: Louis Arnaud, former French hostage in Iran, describes Evin prison as 'the den of evil'

title: European Parliament calls to define rape as sex without consent
content: A resolution adopted by the European Parliament calls for a common definition of rape across the EU, stating that any sexual act without consent should be considered rape.

title: Which European countries have the most teenage entrepreneurs?
content: Around 69,000 EU entrepreneurs are under 20. The Netherlands and Italy lead the pack — a positive sign for Rome, amid gloomy youth employment rates.
snippet: Around 69,000 EU entrepreneurs are under 20. The Netherlands and Italy lead the pack — a positive sign for Rome, amid gloomy youth employment rates.

title: Watch: Champions League semi-finals: When football meets global capital

title: Malta's Prime Minister Robert Abela calls early general election for May

title: EU energy mix: Crisis pushes balance on nuclear, renewables and security
